cot_prefix: Okay, let's tackle this question. The question is asking what made Woolworth a world-famous man. The options are A to D. Let me read through the passage again to make sure I understand the key points. First, the passage starts by telling us about Frank W. Woolworth's humble beginnings. He was born into a poor farming family and decided not to become a farmer. He took a business course and became a salesman. His initial insight was about displaying goods to attract customers. The pivotal moment was when he sold odds and ends at five cents each, which was successful. Then he opened his own store with five and ten-cent goods. He learned that to sell low-price goods, you need to buy in bulk directly from factories. The example given is his large order of knives in Germany, which allowed him to cut costs. By 1919, he had many stores and expanded internationally. He became wealthy and famous, following the rule "The customer is always right."
